topic: Salesforce Agentic Work Units (AWU) Explained for Developers

key_points: Official Salesforce definition: AWU is one discrete task accomplished by an AI agent
AWUs measure total volume of work platform performs on behalf of customers
Tokens measure how much an AI talks; AWUs measure the work it actually completes
The ratio of tokens to AWUs is an 'inference efficiency ratio'
More AWUs from fewer tokens = more value delivered
AWU introduced by Marc Benioff on Feb 25, 2026 Q4 FY2026 earnings call
AWU measures execution rather than accuracy — a triggered workflow counts even if agent failed
Developers should combine AWU metrics with outcome-based measurement
Three pricing models: Flex Credits, per-conversation ($2), per-user ($125/month)
AWU as industry-wide measurement standard push by Salesforce
Flex Credits: 20 credits ($0.10) per standard action, 30 credits ($0.15) per voice action
Credits purchased at $500 per 100,000
Each standard action includes up to 10,000 tokens before multiplier kicks in
Typical conversation involves 5-15 actions costing $0.50-$1.50
AWU is elastic — more work per token as implementations improve
Salesforce spent $300M on Anthropic AI tokens to power agents
AWU correlates tokens consumed with operational value created
Platform has processed 19+ trillion AI tokens → 2.4B AWUs
In Q1 alone: 1.6B AWUs, up 111% quarter-over-quarter
Service Agents: 232M Q1 AWUs (+66% QoQ), Employee Agents: +148% QoQ
Critical view: AWU measures machine exertion, not enterprise value
Agent can execute thousands of tasks while achieving nothing commercially
Comparison to 'bad query' metric in earlier SaaS era
AWU measures activity counts, not quality or business outcomes
Developers must supplement AWU with real outcome tracking

stats.fact: Salesforce has generated 3.8 billion AWUs to date, with 1.6 billion in Q1 FY2027 alone — up 111% quarter-over-quarter
stats.source: Salesforce Q4 FY2026 Earnings Call, Feb 25, 2026

stats.fact: The Salesforce platform has processed over 19 trillion AI tokens, translating to 2.4 billion Agentic Work Units
Service Agents grew 66% QoQ to reach 232M Q1 AWUs; Employee Agents experienced 148% QoQ increase
Slack AI grew 347% quarter-over-quarter driven by Slackbot usage growth
Developer work items completed per developer increased 50.8% YoY (April 2026 vs April 2025); PRs merged per developer up 79% YoY
Agentforce Flex Credits pricing: standard action = $0.10 (20 credits); voice action = $0.15 (30 credits); credits at $500 per 100,000
Typical Agentforce conversation involves 5-15 actions, costing $0.50-$1.50 under Flex Credits
Each standard action includes processing up to 10,000 tokens (~8,000 words); exceeding this counts the action as multiple actions
Salesforce has shipped three different Agentforce pricing models in roughly 18 months: $2/conversation, Flex Credits ($0.10/action), and per-user licenses ($125/user/month)

angles: AWU as a paradigm shift: outcome-based AI billing vs. token-based consumption — what this means for developers budgeting AI projects
The 10,000-token-per-action ceiling and how poor prompt design silently doubles or triples your AWU bill
Developer survival guide: monitoring, alerting, and optimizing Agentforce AWU usage with Agentforce Observability tools
AWU controversy unpacked: why some analysts call it the 'bad query' metric of the AI era — and why developers should care
Choosing the right pricing model: when Flex Credits beats $2/conversation and when per-user licensing wins at scale
From tokens to AWUs: how Salesforce's elastic efficiency goal changes how you architect agents (fewer LLM calls, batching, async processing)
